summary: Matched a large slice of the barrel cannon item implementation in src/melee/it/items/ittarucann.c, replacing many placeholder /// function stubs with C for Tarucann spawn, animation, physics, collision, thrown/dropped, launch, and cleanup behavior. The PR also expanded itTarucann_ItemVars and itTaruCann_DatAttrs in itCommonItems.h, corrected it_8026D564 to return bool, and updated ittarucann.h prototypes. The decomp.dev bot reported +5500 matched bytes, +0.14% matched code, 23 new 100% matches, and 4 near-matches/improvements in the ittarucann unit.

key_files.role: Main implementation; added 541 lines replacing many ittarucann stubs with matched or near-matched C for barrel cannon item logic.
Expanded itTarucann_ItemVars and itTaruCann_DatAttrs from padding/partial fields into offset-annotated fields used by the matched code.
Updated function prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect signatures to concrete Item_GObj*, bool, void, and float signatures.
Changed it_8026D564 from void to bool and returned the floor-collision test result used by ittarucann collision code.
Kept the it_8026D564 declaration in sync with the corrected bool return type.

what_changed: Implemented it_80295ED4, which records the grabbed victim into ip->xDD4_itemVar.tarucann.x20, clears xDD0_flag.b5, calls it_80274ECC, and transitions through it_80297790.

Implemented it_80295F38, which builds lbColl_80008D30_arg1 launch parameters from itTaruCann_DatAttrs fields, normalizes an angle based on M_PI_2 plus the cannon rotation, computes an 8-unit launch position with cosf/sinf, calls ftCo_800C92E4, then calls it_802975F4.

Implemented it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned initialization: facing_dir, flags, itcmd vars, tarucann item vars, random initial angle/range with HSD_Randf, lifetime setup via it_80275158, and initial motion via it_80296694.

Implemented the shared lifetime/visibility helper it_802961E8, decrementing xD44_lifeTimer only when no fighter is stored in tarucann.x20, destroying at timeout, blinking/hiding near expiry, and returning whether the item should terminate.
Implemented motion animation wrappers for motions 0, 1, 6, 7, 8, and 9, including repeated xDCC_flag.b3 updates based on whether tarucann.x20 is null.

Implemented Tarucann collision behavior for ground, air, wall/ceiling flags, slope normals, bounce thresholds, velocity reflection, and motion transitions across itTarucann_UnkMotion0_Coll, itTarucann_UnkMotion1_Coll, itTarucann_UnkMotion6_Coll, and itTarucann_UnkMotion8_Coll.

Implemented rolling/rotation physics helper it_802962E0 and repeated rotation application through inline_itTarucann_SetRotationZ, using tarucann.x10 speed, tarucann.x14 collision normal, tarucann.x28 multiplier, facing_dir, and dat attrs x8/xC/x10/x14/x18/x1C/x50.
Implemented drop/throw/motion-transition helpers it_3F14_Logic5_Dropped, it_3F14_Logic5_Thrown, it_802969D8, it_80296EA8, it_80296EF0, it_802975F4, and it_80297790.
Added includes for camera, effects, ftCo_Barrel, ftlib, it_266F, lb_00B0, lbvector, MSL math, baselib jobj, and baselib random to support the matched code.

Expanded itTarucann_ItemVars from a padding blob plus u32 x20 to typed fields at ip+DD4 through ip+DFC, including rotation/speed floats, a Vec3 normal, a Fighter_GObj* victim pointer, an integer timer/state field, and a float multiplier.

Expanded itTaruCann_DatAttrs through offset +0x50 to expose floats and integer/u32 parameters used for thresholds, acceleration, bounce, damage, knockback angle, element, launch velocity, multiplier, and rotation drift.
Corrected it_8026D564 to return the bool test from it_8026D9A0 and updated callers to branch on it_8026D564(gobj) == 0.
smart_moves: The PR unlocked many functions by first making the item vars and special attributes explicit; the matched implementation depends heavily on offset-accurate tarucann.x* and da->x* fields.
Changing it_8026D564 to return bool was a small cross-file correction that made it usable as a collision/floor test instead of duplicating collision logic in ittarucann.c.
Repeated code for resetting/applying JObj rotation was isolated in inline_itTarucann_SetRotationZ, preserving the two-step HSD_JObjSetRotationZ sequence visible in the diff.

The code reused common item helpers rather than reimplementing behavior: Item_80268E5C for motion changes, it_8026B3A8 for state refresh, it_80274F28 with ftCo_Barrel_Enter for barrel entry callbacks, and it_8026D62C for collision transition handling.

The match kept compiler-sensitive artifacts where needed, including PAD_STACK annotations and awkward temporary-variable patterns in math-heavy functions such as it_802962E0 and itTarucann_UnkMotion7_Phys.
The implementation recognized duplicated animation logic across several motions and factored the motion 9 variant into itTarucann_UnkMotion9_Anim_inline.
Header prototypes were tightened alongside implementation work, reducing future UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS ambiguity for this item.
decomp_lessons: For item actors with many placeholder functions, expanding xDD4 item vars and special attributes can be the key enabling step; otherwise the implementation becomes unreadable or impossible to match.
When a shared helper is known to produce a condition used by a caller, verify its return type globally. Here, a void collision helper became bool and enabled direct branching in item collision code.
Item motion callbacks often share small flag-maintenance blocks. Matching may benefit from local inline helpers, but only when the generated code agrees with the original inlining pattern.
Do not over-name data attributes prematurely when semantics are partial. This PR retained x0/x8/xC/etc. names while still documenting exact offsets and types.
Physics/collision item matches may require explicit absolute-value patterns, temporary variables, and PAD_STACKs to keep instruction scheduling and stack layout aligned.

Use existing engine helpers for item state changes, collision transitions, sound/effect/camera events, and fighter interactions; matching item code tends to mirror engine call structure rather than open-coding behavior.

Near-matches are still valuable when they expose correct structure layout and most control flow; the bot reported it_802962E0 at 99.65% and it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned at 99.40%, indicating remaining compiler/detail differences rather than wholesale uncertainty.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variables use the existing xDD4_itemVar.tarucann namespace with offset-derived field names such as x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x20, x24, and x28.
Special attributes are kept as itTaruCann_DatAttrs fields named by offset, e.g. x0, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, x20, x24, x28, x2C, x30, x34, x38, x3C, x40, x44, x48, x4C, and x50.
Item callback names follow the project’s address/motion naming style: it_80295ED4, it_802962E0, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned, and itTarucann_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll.
Local inline helpers were named with an inline_ prefix, e.g. inline_itTarucann_SetRotationZ and inline_itTarucann_UnkMotion7_Phys.
Function signatures in headers should use concrete return and parameter types once known, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with void, bool, float, and Item_GObj* as appropriate.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(12), and PAD_STACK(16) in several functions to preserve stack layout for matching.
Kept explicit absolute-value branches instead of library fabsf calls, matching codegen patterns around velocity and slope-normal calculations.
Preserved intermediate temporaries such as temp_f1, temp_f2, var_f0, var_f1, var_f2, new_var, and new_var2 in it_802962E0 to influence register allocation and branch shape.
Used inline functions for repeated motion/rotation logic where the original appears to inline rather than call an out-of-line helper.
Called HSD_JObjSetRotationZ twice in inline_itTarucann_SetRotationZ, first with 0.0f and then with tarucann.xC, matching the visible sequence in the diff.
Used M_PI, M_PI_2, cosf, and sinf with explicit angle normalization loops in it_80295F38 instead of simplifying the math.

Preserved item collision flag masks such as 0x18000, 0x3F, and 0xFC0 and event/sound IDs such as 0x104, 0x129, 0x12A, 0xFB, 0x421, and effect 0x427 as raw constants because the PR evidence did not introduce symbolic names for them.

review_feedback.text: Reported matched code 52.38% (+0.14%, +5500 bytes), with 23 new 100% matches in main/melee/it/items/ittarucann.
Reported 4 improvements but not full matches: it_802962E0 at 99.65%, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned at 99.40%, .sdata2 at 94.44%, and .sdata at 89.66%.
No human review comments or review-thread feedback were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Search for remaining non-100% ittarucann functions after PR2161, especially it_802962E0 and it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned.
Search for later PRs renaming itTaruCann_DatAttrs fields from offset names to semantic names.
Search for other callers of it_8026D564 to confirm the bool return type did not require additional callsite updates.
Search for item implementations using inline SetRotationZ helpers or duplicate xDCC_flag.b3 animation logic as reusable matching patterns.
Search for later cleanup of raw event, sound, effect, and collision flag constants introduced or preserved in ittarucann.c.
